evidence: "I really liked this movie! This is a gem of an independent film! What a great story!"
"I found this to be a decent thriller."
"I like movies where I don't feel like I'm being manipulated to an emotional response but where the sentiment just flows naturally from the events."
"I thought it could have been better in many respects but I still thought it was good."
"The story gets going quickly--about two minutes into the film you are already rooting for these two kids!"
"Unlike many films which take forever to get going, rest assured, this one gets going pretty quickly."
"I was surprised at how good the production and direction was and how realistic everything looked for a film made in 1943."
"The picture on the dvd was ok (not great) but the sound wasn't that good and so it was a little hard to hear all of the dialogue."
"I have seen many John Wayne films over the last few years and I have to say I'm not really a fan of his in general."
"I liked that it showed me something I've never thought much about before—that is, the plight of those who lived in the border states during the Civil War."
"It immerses you in the time period of Bernadette's life and the filming of the convent and its surroundings are very well done."
"It shows how the power of suffering can lead us into deeper union with God."
"I liked that it showed me something I've never thought much about before—that is, the plight of those who lived in the border states during the Civil War."

summary: The user enjoys films with strong character development and emotional depth, particularly in the drama and independent genres. They prefer fast-paced narratives with high production quality and a focus on atmosphere, while showing a moderate sensitivity to brand loyalty.
